I pulled over because I saw either a Common Nighthawk or Poorwill and was hoping to photo it, but it flew off as soon as I got out of my car. So all I was left with was bats :)

I don't expect to get a confirmation of these due to the lack of detail, but it was fun practicing low light/action shots just the same. And they make for really neat silhouettes :)

Flew into the garage and up into the stairwell. Got a decent pic then netted with a butterfly net and brought it down. It disentangled itself quickly and after flying in and out a couple times, flew out the garage door. Motion lights on the front of the garage attract flying insects at night and typically several bats can be observed swooping around. This year seems to be the exception, this is the first bat I've seen although the motion lights have been being set off by something when there are no people, pets or wildlife visible.

The bats started streaming out of the roost around 7:59pm (18 minutes after sunset) and continued through 8:06pm (they paused their take-off for a few minutes when a human apartment tenant came out onto the balcony and turned on a light).  We counted about 30 of them streaming out in total.

This is my attempt to take photos of them with an SLR camera.  They fly pretty fast; I was surprised to get anything in the frame.
